title: zabelia buddleioides var. divergens
intro: Zabelia buddleioides var. divergens, also known as the Korean boxwood or Korean lilac, is a type of flowering plant in the family Caprifoliaceae, characterized by its compact growth habit and small, fragrant flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Zabelia buddleioides var. divergens include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can help to prevent infection and promote overall health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
